description: B600N and B600S are parallel intakes (north/south spine) not series. naming_index_chain wrongly inferred parent-child. Removed via topology_overrides.
Patched from 9 children's readings (sum aggregation). Counter froze Jan 17 then reset to 0 on Feb 2.

Counter flat for 6 days — summer shutdown. Self-recovering.
Single-day flat reading (delta=0). Next day shows 200 MWh (two days accumulated).
Device replacement. Old counter ended at 19693. New device started at 1934. Stitched via rolling_sum derived ref.
Counter reset to 0.001 and stayed dead. Patched from child B642.VMM71.
Counter dropped from 25231 to 10890 for 3 days then reverted. Temporary register glitch. Readings excluded via validity split.
B642.VMM72 flow exceeds parent B614.VMM71 by 2-7x during Jan and Apr-Jun. PDF confirms downstream. Suspected undocumented additional steam feed. Needs on-site investigation.

Device swap on 2025-07-31 (Δ=-31533). Originally patched from VMM71 via sum-aggregation, which masked real post-swap readings. Replaced with a third raw segment (:d.C from 2025-08-01) and rolling_sum stitching A|B|C. Jan 2026 now reads 88.87 MWh (vs Excel 89.04; 0.2%). Previously reported 54 MWh (incorrectly mirroring VMM71).

Counter offline + catch-up flushes on 2025-03-20 (+368.6 MWh) and 2025-03-24 (+145.4 MWh). Patched via interpolate between clean endpoints 2025-03-09 (20262.049) and 2025-03-25 (20814.775); 16-day span → ~34.5 MWh/day. Cumulative preserved, daily rate smoothed.

Provider/utility meter with 3 counter resets (~quarterly). All segments stitched via rolling_sum.
Counter drops and stays dead from Feb 12. Leaf meter — cannot patch.
Counter at zero for entire observation window. Sub-feeder of B616.T33 serving B835.
B665.T42-2-1 reads ~5 MWh/month in Snowflake but Excel STRUX_data shows 0. B664 shows excel=0 vs topology=4.9 MWh. B665 overcredited by ~5 MWh/month. Stale STRUX_data.
Sub-feeder subtracted from B659 but not attributed to any building. ~8 MWh/month unaccounted.

Resolved 2026-04-27: B611.T4-A3/C1/C4 ARE in PME after all, under DB-key aliases B611.T1-4-11/12/13 (display names match the cable-position IDs per DATALOG_ENRICHED). Crosswalk was using the display names as snowflake_id, which didn't resolve. Fixed crosswalk to point at the DB keys; B611/B613/B631 now match Excel within 0.7%.

B665.T42-2-1 has live Snowflake readings (~5 MWh/month) attributed to B664 per Excel formula, but Excel cache for B664 = 0 — STRUX_data value is stale/zero while Snowflake reads real consumption. Same pattern as B616 steam.

H23-1 and H3-1 show inverse May-June deviation from the steady ~70/30 ratio (H3-1 peaks +40%, H23-1 dips ~15%). Total intake rises, so not cross-wiring — consistent with H3-1 carrying summer-only cooling/ventilation loads.

Suspected Excel bug in gtn.xlsx / formula_document.xlsx: B612 row 12 subtracts B652.T8-A3-A14-112 at col AA, in addition to already subtracting its parent B641.T8-A3 at col Z. This double-counts the deduction (~21 MWh/month, B612 understated ~4% in Excel). The ontology previously mirrored this via an extra hasSubMeter edge B612.T8 → B652.T8-A3-A14-112; that edge was removed 2026-04-21 so the ontology is physically honest (single parent B641.T8-A3) and the Excel gap is exposed in reconciliation as +21 MWh/month at B612. Location to inspect/fix: gtn.xlsx, B612 row 12, delete col AA term.

GTN EL reconciliation rows marked 'match' may show small drifts at the building level due to Excel cached aggregation vs PME daily-sum rounding. Classifier uses 0.25% relative threshold (raised from 0.1% on 2026-04-27 to absorb noise-level drifts that aren't real discrepancies).
Resolved 2026-04-27: B611.T4-C1 (Excel allocation lands at B613) was thought to be STRUX-only but is in PME under DB-key alias B611.T1-4-12. Crosswalk fix → B613 matches Excel to ±0.7%.

Resolved 2026-04-27: B611.T4-A3 / B611.T4-C4 (Excel allocations land at B631) were thought to be STRUX-only but are in PME under DB-key aliases B611.T1-4-11 / B611.T1-4-13. Crosswalk fix → B631 matches Excel to ±0.7%.

B652.T14-B3-J reads at building B602 per building_id attribution, but Excel formula subtracts it from B652 (row 33 col T) and adds it to B602. Net campus total is unaffected; per-building attribution differs. Open: decide whether to re-attribute the meter to match Excel or add a topology_override.
Resolved 2026-04-27: 0.2% drift was below the new 0.25% noise floor. Now auto-classifies as 'match'. PME daily-sum vs Excel cached aggregation drift, not a real discrepancy.

GTN EL building formulas cross-checked against reference formula doc. All formulas match except: extra +B616.T31-6-2 term removed from B616 pool. Doc attributes B616.T33-6-1/T33-6-2 to B850, but Excel cache records them under B835 — Excel is canonical, ontology retains B835 attribution (gtn_el_b835_vs_b850 annotation documents the divergence).

Reference formula doc lists B850 = +B616.T33-6-1 +B616.T33-6-2 (implying B850 as the owner building). Excel cache keeps the accounting under B835 (~3089/3185 kWh Jan/Feb); B850 Excel = 0. Ontology retains the Excel-canonical B835 attribution. Rename to B850 was attempted and reverted — doc reflects intent, Excel is facit.

Reference formula doc uses B665.T42_2_03 for B664 and B665 formulas. That ID does not exist in Snowflake; B665.T42-2-1 does. Ontology retains the Snowflake-backed B665.T42-2-1. Suspected doc typo; needs upstream verification.

B616.T33-6-1 flat at zero across the full reference period. Existing B616_T31-6-2_offline annotation covers a related sibling meter. B616's T33 branch feeds B850/B835 (doc-vs-Excel naming divergence already annotated). Inactive sub-meter; accepted.

~8.7% of campus EL intake (averaged over 2025-01 to 2026-02) is not captured by any building-level meter. Lifetime: B660.EL_INTAKE = 98646 MWh, sum of buildings = 90041 MWh, residual = 8605 MWh (8.7%). Seasonal pattern: 3-5% in winter, climbing to 11-14% at the July peak. Likely composition: cable/transformer losses (~3-5% of throughput), B660 substation ancillaries (small), outdoor/perimeter lighting fed direct from MV bus, and a couple of buildings present in PME but not in Excel allocations (B648 ~177 MWh/mo, B669 ~22 MWh/mo) accounting for ~30% of the residual. Visible as the gap between the two lines in the Campus conservation chart.

Meter offline 2025-07-30 (zero delta) with catch-up flush on 2025-07-31 (1875 m³, 2.5× median). Patched via interpolate between clean endpoints 2025-07-29 and 2025-07-31, splitting the 2-day accumulation evenly (~937 m³/day). Monthly July total preserved.

Meter offline 2025-08-31 (zero delta) with catch-up flush on 2025-09-01 (1750 m³, 2.4× median). Patched via interpolate between clean endpoints 2025-08-30 and 2025-09-01, splitting the 2-day accumulation evenly (~875 m³/day). Crosses month boundary — August gains ~875 m³ and September loses ~875 m³ vs raw, but combined Aug+Sep total preserved. Outside Excel comparison window (2026-01/02) so Excel comparison is NOT affected.

All GTN KALLVATTEN building formulas cross-checked verbatim against reference/reference_formula_doc/the_only_one_formula_doc.xlsx (KALLVATTEN sheet). Every metered building (39/39) matches current meter_allocations including cross-building subtractions (B615 subtracted from B614; B622+B623 subtracted from B621; B637+B638 subtracted from B612; B613 subtracted from B611 and B612; B626 subtracted from B821; B834 subtracted from B833) and the unusual +B611.VV1_VM21 hot-water term rolled into B611 KV. B843 N/A correctly absent.

Reference formula doc defines a billing-reconciliation aggregator AVRÄKNINGSMÄTARE = B653.KV1_VM21_V + B653.KV1_VM22_V + B654.KV1_VM22_V + B661.KV1_VM21 + B921.KV1_VM21_V. These meters measure water that enters the KV system but evaporates via V2/V9 cooling/process loops (never reaches sewer), so they are deducted from the Telge sewerage fee. Ontology models this as virtual meter GTN.KV_AVRAKNING (campus-level, KALLVATTEN, is_virtual_meter=True). Aggregator CURRENTLY sums 4 of 5: B654.KV1_VM22_V excluded (see gtn_kv_b654_vm22_flat — functionally dead, ~0 contribution). B661.KV1_VM21 participates via its stitched :d ref (see gtn_kv_b661_vm21_resets — 3-segment rolling_sum across two resets). The 5 meters are NOT added to their host-building KV consumption.

B626 is a temporary cabin site ("Bod-etablering") located outside B621. KV consumption (+B626.KV1_VM20 +B626.KV1_VM21) is subtracted from B821 (which houses the supply loop), per reference formula doc.

B656.KV1_VM21 is manually read ("ej uppkopplad men finns i PME, läses in manuellt via EBO") per reference formula doc. No live BMS feed; PME values come from manual Excel entry. Ontology reads from Snowflake if a row exists; otherwise topology shows zero.

B869 is not connected to BMS ("Ej uppkopplad") and its supply valve is expected to be a closed NC-valve per reference formula doc. Any non-zero B869.KV1_VM21 reading indicates either a BMS connection has appeared or the valve leaks. Currently relies on STRUX entry (identifier=strux=B869.KV1_VM21).

Two hard counter drops on B661.KV1_VM21: 2025-11-20 (14572.56 → 7664.02, Δ=-6908.54) and 2026-02-02 (8275.66 → 0.00, Δ=-8275.66). Likely device swap or manual reset events. Handled by 3-segment split :d.A (valid_to=2025-11-20) | :d.B (2025-11-20 → 2026-02-02) | :d.C (from 2026-02-02) stitched into :d via rolling_sum — matches the B616/B642 pattern used elsewhere. Meter also has 147-day max flat-run (counter reports no consumption); keep monitoring.

B654.KV1_VM22_V reports near-flat over 14 months: 402/423 days with zero delta, max 246-day flat run, total +64 m³ on a counter starting at 2041 m³ (~3 m³/day on the 21 active days, 0 on rest). Functionally dead or calibration-floored below 1 m³/day. Excluded from the GTN.KV_AVRAKNING aggregator (its ~64 m³ total is within noise and would add no signal). Raw ref retained for audit. Needs on-site verification whether the circuit is physically in use.

B653.KV1_VM22_V is monotonic but intermittent: 215/423 days flat (51%), max 117-day flat run, no negatives, cumulative +13225 m³ over 14 months. Consumption arrives in bursts after long silent windows — typical BMS gateway outage then catch-up flush pattern. Included in GTN.KV_AVRAKNING as-is (cumulative is correct; only daily-resolution views will show bursts).

B841 KV meters (VM21/22/23/24) are flat or near-zero for most of the reference period (338+ days flat on VM21, 423 days flat on VM22/VM23). Building appears inactive or has minimal cold-water demand. Excel cache values ~0 confirm this.

Several GTN KV meters show 144-day flats ending on 2025-05-25 (B614.KV1_VM22, B650.KV1_VM21, B651.KV1_VM21). Shared recovery date suggests a BMS gateway or SCADA data-collection outage that resolved around late May 2025. Meters have resumed normal reporting; consumption during the outage not recoverable from these meters alone.

B616 KV meters VM21, VM22, VM23 show coincident ~53-day flats ending 2025-12-08. Shared pattern suggests a building-level BMS outage. Meters resumed normal reporting afterward; Dec reconciliation for B616 may be partial.

B850.KV1_VM21 flat for 75 days ending 2025-11-03. Low-magnitude meter (median ~1.7 m³/day, max ~5). Likely a summer/fall dormancy period for the 850/662 building. Excel cache shows small values; impact on reconciliation is minor.

B625.KV1_VM22_V flat ~20 days ending 2025-06-29 then a large single-day catch-up (~266 m³). Monotonic counter retained; no patch applied because the spike is outside the Jan/Feb 2026 reference period and total is preserved. Note for historical analyses.
B654.KV1_VM21_V near-flat late Dec 2025 then a 74 m³ single-day reading on 2026-01-01. Monotonic counter; no patch needed. Could indicate a month-boundary batching artifact.

B600-KB2 is the south-distribution KYLA pool with 3 + inputs in Excel: B623.KB1_INT_VERK1, B653.KB2_WVÄRME_ACK, B654.KB2_Pkyl_Ack. Of these: B654 stays flat all year, B653 is an accumulator (not monotonic), and only B623 consistently contributes. Pool topology mirrors Excel, but B600-KB2 readings reflect only ~1/3 of intended inputs. Building-level reconciliation matches Excel within noise because Excel cache uses the same inputs.

B611 kyla = -20.88 MWh Jan / -12.01 MWh Feb is a data quality artifact. Formula: 0.38×B600-KB2 − B631.KB1_INTE_VERK2 − B631.KB1_VMM51_E. With B600-KB2 pool collapsed to just B623 (see campus annotation "b600kb2_pool_dead_inputs"), the 0.38 share is far smaller than the sub-meters being subtracted. Topology matches Excel exactly. Would revert to plausible positive if B653 / B654.KB2 came back online.

Resolved 2026-04-28 by the same fix as b612_641_fractional_subtraction: coefficient hasSubMeter plus a daily-fill derived timeseries for B612.KB1_PKYL. B641 now matches Excel exactly in Jan; Feb has a residual ~0.04 MWh from the trailing-single-day gap. See b612_641_fractional_subtraction for details.

Resolved 2026-04-28 via daily-fill derived timeseries B833.KB1_GF4:d.daily (aggregation=interpolate, valid 2025-09-08 to 2026-02-28). Source endpoints are both 11439 (counter flat since Sept 2025), giving daily delta=0 and a daily heartbeat row for B833.KB1_GF4. B834 sub now fires every day. B833 matches Excel exactly in Jan; Feb has a trailing-single-day gap of ~0.03 MWh that resolves once a March 2026 raw reading lands.

B658.KB2_VMM51_E reports ~10-12 MWh/month of real cooling in Snowflake, but Excel cache for B658 kyla = 0 (STRUX_data value is stale/zero). Same pattern as B616 steam and B665.T42-2-1 EL — known "Excel=0 but meter live" misallocation. Topology reports the real consumption.

B623.KB1_INT_VERK1 reads cooling flow at the south distribution point, not B623 building's own consumption. Excel uses it twice: as B623's own row (row 23) and as a + input to the B600-KB2 distribution pool (row 8). The pool then sends 100 percent of its flow onward to B611, B613, B621 and B622, so the same energy gets counted twice in Excel's per-row totals. The ontology can only put the meter in one place, so we attach it to the pool (where the flow physically goes) and leave B623 at 0. Excel shows 3.67 MWh (Jan) and 5.06 MWh (Feb) on its B623 row; we accept that gap as a known double-count.

Resolved 2026-04-28. Excel splits the B612 cooling plant remainder (B612.KB1_PKYL − B637 − B638) 90/10 between B612 and B641. Two changes implemented to make the ontology match exactly: (1) views.sql now respects flow_coefficient on hasSubMeter relations, so we attach B637/B638 under B612.KYLA_VIRT at k=0.9 and under B641.KYLA_VIRT at k=0.1; (2) added a daily-fill derived timeseries B612.KB1_PKYL:d.daily (aggregation=interpolate, valid 2025-09-12 to 2026-02-28) so B641.KYLA_VIRT receives a daily heartbeat row and the daily B637/B638 fractional sub fires on every day. B612 and B641 both match Excel within rounding, except a trailing-single-day gap on 2026-02-28 (about 0.04 MWh into B641's column) until the next raw reading lands and we can extend valid_to.

Meter reports power (kW). Excel row 26 converts to MWh via *24*31/1000 (literal 31-day month assumption; coefficient = 0.744). Encoded as feeds B634.KB1_PKYL → B634.KYLA_VIRT at k=0.744, with the residual 0.256 going to a campus-attributed virtual B634.KYLA_CONVERSION_RESIDUAL so outgoing feeds sum to 1.0 (validator requirement). Matches Excel Jan; Feb will be ~11% high vs physical reality because Excel uses the 31-day constant for all months.
Excel row 50: B821 = B821.KB2_VMM51 − 0.8 × B841.KB2_VM51. Fractional subtraction not topologically encoded; skipped because both meters report 0 delta during Jan-Feb 2026. Revisit when active.

Prod-600 is an Excel accounting virtual (total chilled-water production) that sums 4 source meters (B653, B654.KB1, B654.KB2, B661.KB1_Pkyl_Ack) at k=1. It has no downstream dependents. Not modeled in topology to avoid double-counting B653 and B654.KB2 which also feed B600-KB2. Preserved in meter_allocations.csv for documentation.

B612.KB1_PKYL and B833.KB1_GF4 are bi-daily BMS sensors (reading only every other day — 31 readings over 59-day Jan+Feb window). Any hasSubMeter edge with these as parent will only subtract child flow on the days the parent has a reading. Affects B612/B641 (via B612.KB1_PKYL) and B833 (via B833.KB1_GF4). The gaps are fractions of the Excel values: B833 topology reports ~half the expected negative.
Kyla drift of ~4.1% vs Excel, both Jan and Feb 2026. Cause not yet classified. Flagged for diagnosis.
Kyla drift of ~4.1% vs Excel, both Jan and Feb 2026. Cause not yet classified. Same pattern as B621; likely shared root cause.

Reference formula doc (KYLA sheet) defines GTN building formulas using a different meter set than the current ontology. Doc uses KB1/KB2_VMM50_E + KB1_VMM51_E style meters (all confirmed present in Snowflake). Current ontology uses KB1_INT_VERK, KB1_PKYL, KB1_KylEffekt_Ack, KB2_Pkyl_Ack and virtual pool meters (B600-KB2, Prod-600, per-building .KYLA_VIRT). Current ontology delivers 86 of 98 building-months matching Excel cache — i.e. Excel accounting uses the same meter set as our ontology, not the doc's VMM50_E meters. Doc's formulas, if applied verbatim, would regress the reconciliation. Likely the doc reflects a planned meter-renaming pass that has not yet propagated to Excel. Retained current allocations; flagged doc divergence for upstream verification.

Reference formula doc marks ALL SNV buildings as N/A for KYLA and notes 'STÄNGDA HUS RÄKNAS SOM ETT' for the closed-house group (B202/203/204/205/209). However, current SNV KYLA ontology has 22 buildings with real allocations and data (derived from the existing SNV Excel workbook, snv_kyla workstream). The doc appears to be incomplete / not yet authoritative for SNV KYLA. Current ontology retained; doc status noted.

Four GTN KYLA meters are flat at zero for the entire reference period: B638.KB1_INT_VERK1, B643.KB1_INT_VERK, B821.KB2_VMM51, B841.KB2_VM51. Either dead counters or cooling branches that never activated. Excel cache mostly = 0 for these buildings' KYLA so impact on reconciliation is minimal. Flagged for on-site verification.

Several GTN KYLA meters have the '_Ack' suffix (Ackumulerat = accumulated) and legitimately go up and down — they are state accumulators, not monotonic counters. Examples: B661.KB1_Pkyl_Ack (34 negative deltas), B653.KB2_WVÄRME_ACK, B674.KB1_PKYL2_Ack, B654.KB2_Pkyl_Ack, B654.KB1_KylEffekt_Ack. Their readings reflect accumulator state, not cumulative energy. The delta-based anomaly scanner flags these but they are by design.
B642.KB1_INT_VERK_1 flat at zero for 252 days ending 2025-09-10. Possibly decommissioned or physically disconnected; picked up activity only very late in the period. B642 KYLA Excel value is minimal.

B661.KB1_INTVERK was flat for 218 days ending 2025-08-07 (first 7 months of 2025). Large-magnitude meter in active season (median 0.4 MWh/day but bursty to 3 MWh/day). Likely commissioned mid-2025 or prior gateway outage. Historical gap; not patched. Confirmed reporting normally since.

B833.KB1_GF4 shows a 90-day max flat run but bursts to 986 MWh/day. This is the bi-daily BMS polling pattern already noted in b833_negative_sparse — the meter reports on a sparse cadence (~31 reads over 59 days). Cumulative is correct; per-day view shows bursts. See b833_negative_sparse for the downstream implications.

B612.KB1_PKYL shows bursty reporting (88-day max flat, max 1556 MWh/day delta). Likely same sparse-polling pattern as B833.KB1_GF4. Cumulative totals remain correct; daily-resolution views show jumps.

B631.KB1_VMM51_E had a 119-day flat ending 2025-09-30 plus 1 negative delta event. Could be a gauge reset or transient offline. Small absolute impact (median 0.2 MWh/day). Historical context only; meter has been reporting healthy daily values throughout the reconciliation window (Dec 2025 onwards).
B658.KB2_VMM51_E flat 70 days ending 2025-03-26. Low-magnitude cooling meter; pre-season dormancy pattern common to chilled-water sub-meters. Accepted.
B623.KB1_INT_VERK1 flat 34 days ending 2026-01-09. Low-magnitude (median 0.16 MWh/day). Winter dormancy on a cooling circuit is expected. Accepted.

Added derived timeseries B612.KB1_PKYL:d.daily to give the bi-daily raw a daily heartbeat. Implementation: aggregation=interpolate, sources=B612.KB1_PKYL:d, valid_from=2025-09-12 (after the Sept counter step), valid_to=2026-02-28. Source endpoints are both 20097 (the counter has been flat since Sept 2025), so every interpolated daily reading is 20097 and every daily delta is 0. The new ref is preferred=True; the raw :d ref stays preferred=False (untouched, just deprioritized). Purpose: lets B641.KYLA_VIRT (fed at k=0.1 from B612.KB1_PKYL) exist on every day so its daily fractional B637/B638 sub fires. Extend valid_to once a March 2026 raw reading lands.

Added derived timeseries B833.KB1_GF4:d.daily to give the bi-daily raw a daily heartbeat, same pattern as b612_pkyl_daily_fill_synthetic. Implementation: aggregation=interpolate, sources=B833.KB1_GF4:d, valid_from=2025-09-08 (after the last counter step), valid_to=2026-02-28. Source endpoints are both 11439 (counter flat since Sept 2025), so daily delta=0. Purpose: lets the B833.KB1_GF4 → B834.KB2_INT_VERK hasSubMeter sub fire on every day instead of only on bi-daily polling days. Extend valid_to once a March 2026 raw reading lands.

B614 kyltornsvatten reports on ~2-day cadence (215 rows / 424 days, ~95% zero-flow). Bursts up to 504k L/day (e.g. 2025-09-06) are cumulative catchups, not spikes. Counter monotonic; total is correct. Daily-resolution views will look bursty.
